item.title: Meta’s Wang Says AI Agents Will Redefine How We Relate to Technology
item.description: The industry needs to build trust for people to make the best use of AI

item.title: Trump’s Tax Cuts Are No Longer ‘Beautiful’ in Republican Rebrand
item.description: Treasury’s Bessent changes messaging on tax cuts in pitch to “working families.”

item.title: UK Sees Risk of $100 Oil Until 2028 in Worsening Iran Outlook
item.description: The UK government has revised its internal oil price forecasts upwards, assessing that crude could stay around $100 a barrel until 2028 even with a US-Iran peace deal because energy flows from the Gulf are now expected to take longer to recover.

item.title: Manchester United Looks at Refinancing $425 Million of Debt
item.description: Manchester United is looking at tapping the private placement market to refinance $425 million of debt due next year, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: India Growth Beats Estimates Despite Energy Costs, Rupee Slide
item.description: India’s economy grew faster than expected in the three months through March, despite rising energy costs linked to the Iran war and a weaker currency.

item.title: Indonesia Faces Crisis of Confidence as Markets Decode Prabowo
item.description: Indonesia spent much of this week confronting a question that might have seemed unthinkable a few short years ago: what happens when investors stop believing in Southeast Asia’s largest economy?

item.title: Chatbots Are Trying to Replace the Work of Financial Advisers
item.description: How much money would you hand to Claude?

item.title: TPG, Oaktree, Oak Hill Lead €1 Billion Funding for Evoke Buyout
item.description: Private lenders including TPG Angelo Gordon International, Oaktree Capital Management and Oak Hill Capital Partners are set to provide around €1 billion ($1.2 billion) of funding for the acquisition of gambling firm Evoke Plc by Bally’s Intralot.

item.title: US Jobs Report Is a Gut Check for Bond Traders
item.description: A weak jobs report could spark bonds rally

item.title: EU Lender Sees Scope for Hungary Lending Boost on Funding Deal
item.description: The European Investment Bank sees scope for significantly expanding its lending to Hungary after newly elected Prime Minister Peter Magyar convinced the European Union to unlock frozen funds, a senior EIB official said.

item.title: Goldman’s Mueller-Glissman Sees Healthy Pullback in Tech Rally
item.description: The recent selloff in technology stocks is a healthy sign after an increase in speculative positioning in the sector, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s head of asset allocation research.

item.title: Algeria Starts Work on Trans-Saharan Gas Pipe to Supply Europe
item.description: Algeria said it started building a pipeline to stretch across the Sahara, a project first proposed decades ago to supply gas from Nigeria and the region to customers in Europe.

item.title: Fed’s Logan Finds More Inflation Drivers in West Texas
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan doesn’t have to look far to see evidence of reigniting price pressures.

item.title: Chinese, HK Investors Banned From SpaceX IPO on Security Grounds
item.description: Underwriters on SpaceX’s $75 billion initial public offering have been told not to accept orders from investors in Hong Kong and China, citing US restrictions around the export of critical technology, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: What to Expect From Apple’s AI, Siri and iOS 27 Launch at WWDC
item.description: The iPhone maker will attempt its artificial intelligence turnaround on Monday.

item.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk to Spend $30 Billion on India Data Centers
item.description: AirTrunk, a data center operator backed by Blackstone Inc., plans to invest about $30 billion in India over the next few years to tap the country’s rapidly expanding AI and cloud markets.

item.title: Apex Space Doubles Valuation to $2.3 Billion in Funding Round
item.description: Startup Apex Space announced that it had raised more than $200 million as it works to increase production of satellite platforms for commercial firms and government work, including the development of President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome missile defense shield.

item.title: Japan PM Says Government Needs More Careful Communication With Market
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s government needs to communicate more carefully with the bond market, she told a parliamentary committee on Friday.

item.title: Euro-Zone Economy Shrank at Start of Year Because of Ireland
item.description: The euro-zone economy shrank at the start of the year after an unprecedented contraction in Ireland forced a revision to data that originally showed feeble growth.

item.title: Anthropic Calls for AI Pause Button to Let Humans Take Stock
item.description: Anthropic PBC called for the creation of an industrywide mechanism to pause the development of artificial intelligence, giving society the opportunity to “deal with its immense implications.”

item.title: Telenor Is Said to Explore Sale of Digital Bank in Pakistan
item.description: Telenor ASA is considering selling its controlling stake in its digital bank in Pakistan, people familiar with the matter said, a move that would mark its full exit from the South Asian nation.

item.title: BOE Survey Shows Firms Plan Price Hikes to Protect Margins
item.description: UK firms expect to raise prices by 4% in the coming year, according to a Bank of England survey that suggests companies are trying to pass on higher energy costs to consumers.

item.title: Taiwan Inflation Tops Central Bank Alert Level on Oil Prices
item.description: Taiwan’s consumer inflation sped past the monetary authority’s alert level, strengthening the case for further rate increases.

item.title: Carmignac Sees Deficits Making Central Banks Inflation Tolerant
item.description: Carmignac is extending the maturity of its inflation-linked bond holdings in a bet that mounting pressure on government budgets will push central banks to tolerate higher inflation in coming years.

item.title: Danantara Plans Low-Yield Bond as Investors Shun Indonesia
item.description: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara is planning to sell local-currency bonds that will pay interest below market rates at a time when global investors are rapidly losing confidence in the nation’s markets.

item.title: Inside Hudson River Trading's Blistering Token Burn
item.description: How the market-maker is spending on AI.

item.title: Global Food Prices Steady Near Highest Level in Three Years
item.description: Global food prices remained near the highest level in more than three years, after a drop in the cost of palm and soy oils countered disruptions to key agricultural input flows from the Iran war.

item.title: Odd Lots: Inside Hudson River Trading’s Token Burn (Podcast)
item.description: Today’s episode, which was recorded at our recent live show at New York’s City Winery, follows up on a conversation we had with Iain Dunning, head of AI at Hudson River Trading. Last year, we talked about how his firm uses AI. Now, some seven months later, we follow up on how one of the biggest market makers around is deploying this technology. We talk about the price of memory, bottlenecks in compute, how much HRT employees are actually spending on tokens, why the firm might develop its own chi

item.title: ECB to Hike Twice With Inflation Above Comfort Zone, Poll Shows
item.description: The European Central Bank will respond to the Iran war by raising interest rates twice this year before them keeping steady for longer than previously thought, economists say.

item.title: Hon Hai Sales Growth Accelerates Beyond Estimates in May
item.description: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co.’s revenue rose 34% in the April to May period, underscoring demand for the Nvidia Corp. servers essential to AI infrastructure.
